name: Thermodynamics and Statistical Mechanics
description: Entropy, phase transitions, fluctuation theorems, maximum entropy

results.name: Second Law of Thermodynamics
results.structural_conclusion: The universe possesses an intrinsic arrow of time through entropy increase, making certain processes irreversible and establishing a fundamental asymmetry between past and future.
results.source_citation: Clausius, R. (1850). Über die bewegende Kraft der Wärme

results.name: Boltzmann's H-theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Macroscopic irreversibility emerges from reversible microscopic dynamics through statistical considerations, bridging the apparent contradiction between time-symmetric mechanics and thermodynamic irreversibility.
results.source_citation: Boltzmann, L. (1872). Weitere Studien über das Wärmegleichgewicht

results.name: Gibbs Ensemble Theory
results.structural_conclusion: Thermodynamic properties emerge from probability distributions over microstates, establishing that macroscopic behavior is fundamentally statistical rather than deterministic.
results.source_citation: Gibbs, J.W. (1902). Elementary Principles in Statistical Mechanics

results.name: Nernst Heat Theorem (Third Law)
results.structural_conclusion: Absolute zero represents a fundamental boundary where entropy approaches a universal constant, establishing quantum mechanics as the underlying structure of thermodynamics at low temperatures.
results.source_citation: Nernst, W. (1906). Über die Berechnung chemischer Gleichgewichte

results.name: Einstein-Smoluchowski Fluctuation Theory
results.structural_conclusion: Thermal fluctuations reveal the discrete molecular nature of matter and connect microscopic randomness to macroscopic observables through precise statistical relationships.
results.source_citation: Einstein, A. (1905). Über die von der molekularkinetischen Theorie

results.name: Onsager Reciprocal Relations
results.structural_conclusion: Time-reversal symmetry at the microscopic level imposes universal constraints on transport coefficients, revealing deep connections between different irreversible processes.
results.source_citation: Onsager, L. (1931). Reciprocal Relations in Irreversible Processes

results.name: Landau Theory of Phase Transitions
results.structural_conclusion: Phase transitions represent symmetry-breaking events where order parameters emerge, establishing symmetry as the fundamental organizing principle of phases of matter.
results.source_citation: Landau, L.D. (1937). On the theory of phase transitions

results.name: Yang-Lee Circle Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: The zeros of the partition function in the complex plane determine all thermodynamic singularities, revealing phase transitions as emergent from analytic structure.
results.source_citation: Yang, C.N. and Lee, T.D. (1952). Statistical Theory of Equations of State

results.name: Fluctuation-Dissipation Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Response to external perturbations and spontaneous fluctuations are universally related, revealing equilibrium as a dynamic balance between opposing processes.
results.source_citation: Callen, H.B. and Welton, T.A. (1951). Irreversibility and Generalized Noise

results.name: Maximum Entropy Principle
results.structural_conclusion: Statistical mechanics emerges from information theory as the unique unbiased inference given constraints, establishing entropy as a measure of missing information rather than physical disorder.
results.source_citation: Jaynes, E.T. (1957). Information Theory and Statistical Mechanics

results.name: Renormalization Group Theory
results.structural_conclusion: Critical phenomena exhibit scale invariance and universality, revealing that near phase transitions, microscopic details become irrelevant and systems organize into universal classes.
results.source_citation: Wilson, K.G. (1971). Renormalization Group and Critical Phenomena

results.name: Jarzynski Equality
results.structural_conclusion: Equilibrium free energy differences can be extracted from non-equilibrium processes, revealing deep connections between equilibrium and non-equilibrium thermodynamics through exponential averaging.
results.source_citation: Jarzynski, C. (1997). Nonequilibrium Equality for Free Energy Differences

results.name: Crooks Fluctuation Theorem
results.structural_conclusion: Forward and reverse process probabilities are related by exponential factors involving entropy production, quantifying time-reversal asymmetry at the trajectory level.
results.source_citation: Crooks, G.E. (1999). Entropy production fluctuation theorem

results.name: Lieb-Robinson Bounds
results.structural_conclusion: Information propagation in quantum many-body systems has a finite speed limit, establishing locality as a fundamental constraint even in quantum statistical mechanics.
results.source_citation: Lieb, E.H. and Robinson, D.W. (1972). The finite group velocity

results.name: Area Law for Entanglement Entropy
results.structural_conclusion: Ground states of gapped local Hamiltonians exhibit entanglement that scales with boundary area rather than volume, revealing how quantum correlations organize spatially in many-body systems.
results.source_citation: Eisert, J., Cramer, M., Plenio, M.B. (2010). Colloquium: Area laws

structural_conclusion: Thermodynamics reveals that macroscopic irreversibility emerges from reversible microscopic dynamics through statistical averaging, with entropy serving as both a measure of missing information and the driver of time's arrow. Phase transitions represent symmetry-breaking organizational events governed by universal scaling laws, while fluctuation theorems expose the deep mathematical structure relating equilibrium and non-equilibrium processes. The field ultimately shows that collective behavior transcends microscopic details through universal principles of information, symmetry, and statistical emergence.
